heb.paragraph: "Vindicate me (shāfṭēnî), LORD, for I have led a blameless life" (v.1). Another innocence plea (cf. Ps 7, 17). David invites examination: test me, try me (v.2). He walks in God’s faithfulness, avoids the deceitful, hates evildoers’ company (vv.3–5). He washes his hands in innocence (v.6).

panels.ctx: David claims integrity and invites God’s examination. He has not sat with the deceitful or associated with hypocrites. He washes his hands — the ritual gesture of innocence (cf. Pilate, Matt 27:24).

cross.note: Pilate washes his hands — the same gesture, without the substance.

notes.note: "Vindicate me, LORD. Test me, try me, examine my heart and my mind." Total transparency before God.
"I wash my hands in innocence." The ritual gesture confirming moral purity.

alter.source: Robert Alter, The Hebrew Bible: A Translation with Commentary — Scholarly Paraphrase

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

sections.header: Verses 7–12 — “I Love the House Where You Live”

heb.paragraph: "LORD, I love the house where you live (məʿōn), the place where your glory dwells" (v.8). The temple is David’s joy. His feet stand on level ground; in the assemblies he praises the LORD (v.12).

panels.ctx: David loves God’s house (v.8), asks not to be swept away with sinners (v.9), and declares: "my feet stand on level ground; in the great congregation I will praise the LORD" (v.12).

cross.note: "One thing I ask: to dwell in the house of the LORD" — the same temple-longing.
"Better is one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere" — the same temple-love.

notes.note: "I love the house where you live." Temple devotion. The physical space matters because God’s glory dwells there.
"My feet stand on level ground." Stability through integrity. The concluding image: firm footing.

alter.source: Robert Alter, The Hebrew Bible: A Translation with Commentary — Scholarly Paraphrase

net.source: NET Bible Full Notes Edition — Biblical Studies Press

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVfor I have always been mindful of your unfailing love and have lived in reliance on your faithfulness.ESVFor your steadfast love is before my eyes, and I walk in your faithfulness.NIVDo not take away my soul along with sinners, my life with those who are bloodthirsty,ESVDo not sweep my soul away with sinners, nor my life with bloodthirsty men,

rec.text: ""Vindicate me, LORD. Test me, try me, examine my heart and my mind." Total transparency before God."

rows.label: The Examination — 26:1–6
rows.text: "Vindicate me." Test, try, examine. Hands washed in innocence.

hebtext.note: "Vindicate me (shāfṭēnî), LORD, for I have led a blameless life" (v.1). Another innocence plea (cf. Ps 7, 17). David invites examination: test me, try me (v.2). He walks in God’s faithfulness, avoids the deceitful, hates evildoers’ company (vv.3–5). He washes his hands in innocence (v.6).
"LORD, I love the house where you live (məʿōn), the place where your glory dwells" (v.8). The temple is David’s joy. His feet stand on level ground; in the assemblies he praises the LORD (v.12).

thread.text: Pilate washes his hands — the same gesture, without the substance.
"One thing I ask: to dwell in the house of the LORD" — the same temple-longing.
"Better is one day in your courts than a thousand elsewhere" — the same temple-love.

themes.note: Worship peaks: love for God’s house. Holiness: the innocence claim and examination. Faith: inviting God’s scrutiny.
